_review: Human/agent visual review of iteration 1. Empty string = output looked right. Focus is whether shadixfy specifically lands the shadcn idiom (not just generic anti-Codex calm, which uncodixfy also achieves).
dashboard/claude/no_skill: Textbook Codex UI: gradient logo blob, gradient New Report/Upgrade buttons, cyan/purple donut, gradient progress bars, glow area chart, sidebar Upgrade CTA. The baseline we're beating.
dashboard/claude/uncodixfy: Big improvement but it's uncodixfy's OWN taste: calm DARK Linear-ish theme, single muted purple accent. Anti-Codex, yes; shadcn, no.
dashboard/claude/shadixfy: On target: light neutral shadcn look — bordered cards, black primary Export button, monochrome chart, real status badges (Trial outline / Active solid / Past due destructive), avatar initials, user switcher at sidebar foot. Reads as shadcn dashboard-01. ONE miss: used backdrop-blur on the sticky top bar (failed the no-glass assertion).
dashboard/codex/shadixfy: On target and agent-consistent with the Claude version: light neutral shadcn idiom, hairline-bordered metric strip, monochrome chart, icon-nav sidebar + user at foot. Slightly more minimal (no colored status badges).
settings-form/claude/shadixfy: Clean shadcn settings: labels above inputs, sober toggles, black Save button, neutral palette. Good.
_iteration_candidates: Sticky-header backdrop-blur: shadixfy/claude used backdrop-blur on the sticky top bar, which the skill's anti-glass rule bans but which is a legit shadcn pattern (bg-background/80 + backdrop-blur). Decide: (a) add an explicit carve-out in the skill for sticky headers, or (b) tighten the rule to forbid it everywhere. Either way the assertion and the skill should agree.

Mechanical assertions don't distinguish shadixfy from uncodixfy (both ~1.0) because they test anti-Codex hygiene, not shadcn conformance. Add shadcn-specific auto-checks for iteration 2: uses hsl(var(--token)) / CSS custom properties for color, neutral base (zinc/neutral/stone) detected, presence of shadcn-style components (badge/card/border tokens). That will turn the visual edge into a measurable one.
